title: scan_block clones entire witness map per block — O(N) deep clone in scan hot path
description: scanner.rs scan_block lines 404-405: tree_snapshot = self.tree.clone() and witnesses_snapshot = self.witnesses.clone() deep-clone the entire witness HashMap before each block. Each IncrementalWitness<sapling::Node, 32> is ~1KB (32 levels × 32 bytes). With 1000 unspent notes this is ~1MB of cloning per block, in the scan hot path. Blocks arrive at 75s intervals on mainnet, making this workload ~800 KB/min in steady state, but during initial sync (downloading years of blocks at network speed) this blows up. The snapshot is only needed if save_block_state fails. Consider copy-on-write or a dirty-flag approach: only snapshot when save_block_state actually fails, or batch witness updates in a shadow map and swap atomically.
